title: lora_l2.c + lichen_l2.c: lichen_l2_send state check has TOCTOU with lora_l2 state
description:
Cross-file interaction bug: lichen_l2_send() checks is_running() but lora_l2.c state can change before TX completes.

lichen_l2.c:891 checks lichen_lora_l2_is_running() before proceeding
lichen_l2.c:980 calls lichen_lora_l2_tx() which checks state again at lora_l2.c:1032-1039

RACE SCENARIO:
1. Thread A: lichen_l2_send() checks is_running()=true at line 891
2. Thread B: lichen_lora_l2_stop() transitions RUNNING->STOPPED
3. Thread A: Proceeds to line 980, calls lichen_lora_l2_tx()
4. lora_l2.c:1037 checks state != RUNNING, returns -ENETDOWN
5. lichen_l2.c:988 returns the error

This is actually HANDLED CORRECTLY - the double-check in tx() catches the race. But the L2 send has already done work (linearizing packet, SCHC compression) that is wasted.

The dual check creates redundancy: lichen_l2.c checks is_running(), then lora_l2.c checks state again. The first check is an optimization to fail-fast.

P4: Not a correctness bug, just inefficiency on stop/send race. Document that the early check is optimization only.
close_reason: not-a-bug: dual check is defense-in-depth, inner check catches race
